tipRules.tip: KVD: 'The bait HAS to hit something — rocks, grass, bottom. Simply retrieving in a straight line won't yield results. If the bait isn't hitting anything, stop reeling and let it sink until it does. Deflection and disturbance trigger strikes.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'For lipless on flats: 7-foot glass rod, 5:1 gear ratio reel. Slower retrieve keeps bait down in the strike zone. Line controls depth — 20lb test for shallow water above grass, 8-10lb fluorocarbon for deeper to maximize depth and bottom contact.'

KVD: 'On large flats, the key is contact, depth, and placement. Vary retrieve speed with stop-and-go movements. Let the bait sink periodically. Always aim for the bait to be hitting something — if it's not interacting with cover, it loses effectiveness.'
